subject: Mozi Minute: How To Reinvest Profits
body:
Words I like: The businesses that reinvest their profits grow exponentially. The ones that don't grow linearly until they plateau.

Mozi Minute: How To Reinvest Profits

Most service businesses hit a wall because they don't know where to reinvest their profits.

On the other hand, manufacturing companies have obvious reinvestment - better equipment, more capacity, R&D.

But when you're selling expertise and time, it's less clear (which today still represents nearly 80% of businesses).

So owners pocket the profit and wonder why growth stalls.

Here's the truth:

You always want to reinvest in whatever gets you the highest return.

For most businesses, that's the business itself. But service businesses have two specific places that extra profit should go.

Marketing & Brand:

This gives you pricing power and retention. When you're known in your market, you can charge 2-3x more than competitors.

Plus, strong brands have lower churn rates.

That future pricing power and retention turn into future profits that compound.

Talent:

This gives you capacity and optionality. Better people can handle bigger clients, start new divisions, or make your company sellable without you.

Each A-player hire multiplies your ability to serve more clients at higher price points.

The math is simple:

A $2M service business that reinvests 30% of profits into marketing and talent for 3 years will outperform the one that pockets the profit every single time.

One builds enterprise value.

The other builds a higher-paying job.

Example: Agency owner making $500k profit.

Option 1: Take it all home.

Option 2: Invest $200k in brand building and $150k in senior talent.

Year three, the first owner still makes $500k.

The second owner's business is worth $5M and throws off $1.2M annually.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Reinvest to multiply, don't extract to survive,

Alex
